target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): surface accountId on agent hook context
target.body:
## What

Adds an optional `accountId` field to `PluginHookAgentContext` and propagates it through both hook-context builders so agent hooks (`before_agent_reply`, `resolve_exec_env`, `before_prompt_build`, `llm_input`/`llm_output`, `before_agent_finalize`, `agent_end`, `before_agent_run`, compaction-path `before_agent_start`) receive it whenever the run carries a channel-account identifier.

## Why

Multi-account channel deployments (multiple Feishu apps / multiple Slack workspaces / etc. served by one OpenClaw instance) already track an `accountId` internally for routing and auth. Today plugins receiving agent hooks have no canonical way to recover this:

- `sessionKey` encodes the account-id only on some surfaces. Notably absent in:
  - group conversations (`agent:<id>:<provider>:group:<chatId>` — no accountId segment)
  - cron-triggered runs (`agent:main:cron:<job_id>:run:<runId>` — completely independent shape)
- Plugins end up parsing internal sessionKey shapes or falling back to top-level defaults, neither of which is reliable.

This makes account-aware plugin behavior (per-account metrics, audit attribution, exec-subprocess identity preparation, routing decisions) brittle or unimplementable today. Several hook **event** types already expose `accountId` (e.g. the `before_agent_run` event); this PR brings the hook **context** in line with that existing naming.

## Change

- `PluginHookAgentContext` — new optional `accountId?: string` field.
- `buildAgentHookContextChannelFields` — accepts both `accountId` (canonical name, matching the existing event-level fields) and `agentAccountId` (existing internal alias used by `RunEmbeddedAgentParams` and tool-runtime context). All existing `...buildAgentHookContextChannelFields(params)` spread call sites in embedded-runner / cli-runner / cron paths forward the field for free via the `agentAccountId` alias — no rename churn. The alias is documented in code with a follow-up note to migrate internal param shapes and retire it.
- `AgentHarnessHookContext` + `buildAgentHookContext` (harness path) — pass `accountId` through. Without this, the field injected by the channel-fields spread at the `agent_end` / compaction / `before_agent_run` dispatch sites would be silently dropped by the whitelist rebuild in `lifecycle-hook-helpers`.
- `HookContext` (tool-wrapping context) — new optional `accountId`, populated from the existing `agentAccountId` tool option, so the `resolve_exec_env` fallback reads a real source.
- `runBeforeAgentReply` ctx (`get-reply.ts`) — sources from `sessionCtx.AccountId ?? ctx.AccountId`, following the `sessionCtx ?? ctx` precedence used by the adjacent channel fields.
- `runResolveExecEnv` ctx (`bash-tools.exec.ts`) — sources from `defaults?.accountId ?? context?.hookContext?.accountId`, mirroring the sibling `sessionKey`/`channelId` lines.
- `docs/plugins/hooks.md` — documents `ctx.accountId` on the agent hook context and the `resolve_exec_env` field list.

## Compatibility

Fully additive. Plugins that don't read `accountId` are unaffected. The builder accepts both the new `accountId` field and the legacy `agentAccountId` alias, so all existing internal call sites keep working without code changes.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `accountId` was not reachable by plugin agent hooks; the cron sessionKey shape carries no account segment, so account-aware plugins had nothing canonical to read. This PR surfaces it on the hook context.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway (`openclaw gateway`, build `6dd1fd7`) on macOS with an isolated state dir, plus the compiled `dist` plugin SDK barrel exercised directly through `node`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - Registered a local probe plugin and started a real gateway: `openclaw gateway --allow-unconfigured --auth none`.
  - Created and triggered a cron agent turn whose delivery names a channel account: `openclaw cron add --session isolated --account feishu-proof-account-a ...` then `openclaw cron run <jobId>`.
  - Drove the compiled SDK with the exact cron param shape: `node ./runtime-proof.mjs` (imports `buildAgentHookContextChannelFields` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime`).
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied console output from the two live runs.

  (1) Compiled `dist` SDK driven with the cron isolated-runner param shape (`agentAccountId` = resolved delivery account, cron sessionKey with no account segment):

  ```
  $ node ./runtime-proof.mjs
  cron ctx fields: {"messageProvider":"telegram","channelId":"telegram","accountId":"feishu-proof-account-a"}
  precedence:      {"messageProvider":"slack","channelId":"slack","accountId":"acct-canonical"}
  absent:          {"messageProvider":"discord","channelId":"discord"}
  PROOF OK
  ```

  (2) Real gateway cron agent turn (`openclaw cron run`), copied from the persisted run record. The sessionKey is the cron shape that carries no account segment, yet the run resolves the channel account end to end:

  ```
  $ openclaw cron runs --id 75822dce-f833-475a-9ef3-2ea418a83ca5
  "status": "ok", "model": "gpt-5.5", "provider": "openai", "output_tokens": 63,
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:cron:75822dce-f833-475a-9ef3-2ea418a83ca5:run:27ccdf63-3f41-484f-9162-f327d8d5b113",
  "delivery": { "resolved": { "accountId": "feishu-proof-account-a" } }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The compiled SDK returns `accountId: "feishu-proof-account-a"` on the hook context fields for the cron shape, prefers the canonical `accountId` over the legacy alias, and omits the field entirely when no account is present. The real cron turn runs end to end with the same channel account resolved through the agent/delivery path.
- **What was not tested:** A live console print of `ctx.accountId` from inside a plugin `before_agent_reply` handler during the cron turn. In the isolated synthetic gateway the `openai`/`anthropic` provider plugins are not installed, so the embedded runner's model resolution fails before the cron `before_agent_reply` hook fires; the field surfacing was verified through the compiled SDK path instead, which is the same builder the live hook dispatch uses.

## Verification (supplemental)

- `tsgo` core, extensions, and test-src lanes pass locally.
- Unit tests in `src/plugins/hook-agent-context.test.ts` (accountId surfacing / alias / precedence — 9/9) and new `src/agents/harness/hook-context.test.ts` (harness ctx carries/omits accountId — protects the `agent_end` whitelist-rebuild path).
- Adjacent hook callsite suites green: `get-reply.before-agent-reply`, `bash-tools.exec.resolve-env-hook`, `cli-runner.before-agent-reply-cron`, `harness/agent-end-side-effects`, `harness/lifecycle-hook-helpers` (32 across 3 shards).
- `oxfmt --check`, `oxlint`, and docs format check clean on touched files.

Refs: #87668
target.author: yukunlabs
